Summary: CNS neuroblastoma with FOXR2 activation (CNS NB-FOXR2) is a molecularly defined CNS tumor entity that corresponded to a relatively pure population of CNS-PNET and was enriched for the CNS-PNET variants classified as CNS neuroblastoma or ganglioneuroblastoma. In CNS NB-FOXR2 tumors FOXR2 is activated and expressed, and FOXR2 is not expressed in other CNS tumor types or normal brain. Genome-wide sequencing identified complex inter- and intrachromosomal rearrangements converging on FOXR2, including fusion transcripts that retain the full FOXR2 coding sequence fused to upstream non-coding exons and partners, tandem duplications, deletions between FOXR2 and MAGEH1, copy-number changes, and a mitochondrial insertion that created a novel FOXR2 promoter. The described alterations led to increased FOXR2 expression in CNS NB-FOXR2 tumors compared with other CNS entities and are described as instigating oncogenic activity. CNS NB-FOXR2 tumors display embryonal architecture with a small-cell phenotype and frequently show neuropil, neurocytic or ganglion cell differentiation, perivascular anuclear zones, nuclear palisades, and Homer Wright rosettes. Tumors in this entity nearly uniformly express OLIG2 and the neuronal antigen synaptophysin.
References: Sturm, D., Orr, B. A., Toprak, U. H., Hovestadt, V., Jones, D. T. W., Capper, D., Sill, M., Buchhalter, I., Northcott, P. A., Leis, I., Ryzhova, M., Koelsche, C., Pfaff, E., Allen, S. Cell, 164(5), 1060-1072. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.01.015
